ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] (Hymns) Listen to Sibelius' tune which was used as the base for our hymn. [Finlandia](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fE0RbPsC9uE)
[ ] (Music Appreciation) Plan a field trip to an orchestra concert. Check on special programs for children. Some orchestras allow children to attend certain rehearsals for a reduced rate. Some provide sensory-friendly opportunities.
[ ] (Music Appreciation) Read Pooh Stories.
[ ] (Sol-fa: Musical Games) Read Pooh Stories.
[ ] (Folk Songs) [Voices Across Time](https://voices.pitt.edu/index.htm)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [Another Japanese Folk Song](https://liuchiuan.com/2014/06/25/asadoya-yunta-a-common-bond-for-okinawans-the-world-over/)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [The appropriation of Cultures by Percival Everett](https://faculty.gordonstate.edu/lsanders-senu/Appropriation of Cultures by Evertt.pdf)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [An old Irish Song which might have been the inspiration for Yankee Doodle](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DGwF4Rvors0)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [Another Famous Japanese folk song about a rabbit](https://www.japanese-song.com/usagi/)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [About Japanese Scales](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ny4mzbbTLAc)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [Yatsuhashi Kengyō](https://www.komuso.com/people/people.pl?person=699)

[ ] (Hymns) Explore the Olney hymnbook that Cowper and Newton published! [Olney Hymns](https://books.google.com/books?id=1P5NAQAAMAAJ&pg=PR2&source=kp_read_button&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0#v=onepage&q&f=false).
[ ] (Hymns) [William Cowper's poetry](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poets/william-cowper#tab-poems)
[ ] (Music Appreciation) Make a [carrot recorder](https://youtu.be/Tn5O3_C1sqw?si=gogBMAT0o93_dJ1I).
[ ] (Folk Songs) [More about Voyageurs](https://www.thecanadianencyclopedia.ca/en/article/voyageur)

[ ] (Hymns) Learn more about [Appalachian Music](https://www.loc.gov/item/ihas.200152683/).
[ ] (Music Appreciation) Listen to the [Wild Symphony playlist](https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_lJtbpFFWQnl1mQch9iN_HJ4Fv8zBmzdck) by Dan Brown.
[ ] (Folk Songs) Listen to lullabies in a language you are studying. [Spanish](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_kEkrg_Qq4JuNMfbwYuI5Oe-fxMKi4sQvA)
[ ] (Folk Songs) Listen to more lullabies from North America! [Under the Green Corn Moon - Native American Lullabies by Tom Wasinger](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Zpb7CWcPeRQ)

resources.title: 150 American Folk Songs to Sing, Read, and Play
resources.author: Peter Erdei (editor), Katalin Komolos (composer)
resources.rationale: Good collection of American folk songs for younger children. Includes some games and sol-fa notes.

resources.title: Kazoos
resources.author: BTOP Store
resources.rationale: Kazoo along with the term's hymn, spirituals, or folk songs as a fun way to practice learning the melody. Can use any kind of kazoo.

resources.title: Mama Lisa's World
resources.author: Mama Lisa
resources.rationale: Free site of folk songs from around the world. Often includes recordings, sheet music, and lyrics (Note: There will be discrepancies among the versions).

resources.title: Sing Your Part
resources.author: Sing Hymns in Parts
resources.rationale: App (accessible for free on a web browser or for $5 on the Apple Store) which teaches all four parts for each hymns. Once you sign up, you'll have to randomly select a church, and then you can search in the song library for a hymn you'd like to learn, adjust the audio for the part you'd like to hear, and then begin singing! Because of copyright restrictions, only older hymns are generally available.
